title: PII PHI PCI classification pass
description: Classify direct identifiers, indirect identifiers, sensitive attributes, health data, payment data, credentials, and derived identifiers before changing flows.
body: Use when a field name, payload, log, or export may contain personal data. Do not make legal conclusions about jurisdictional status. Steps: classify fields by identifiability and sensitivity; note aggregation or pseudonymization limits; identify regulated subclasses such as PHI or PCI; and choose the strictest applicable engineering control. Validation signals include classification notes and tests preventing raw values in unauthorized sinks. Risk boundary: uncertain classification of sensitive data triggers human review. Use when Privacy Risk Controls work touches personal-data classes, sinks, retention, consent, logs, prompts, and exports. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: follow the ordered review, edit, and validation sequence; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include data inventory, redaction tests, retention checks, export/delete tests, and approval evidence. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves PII disclosure, consent bypass, retention drift, and unapproved processor expansion, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
